response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior engineer but the profile is a poor match for this C++/Windows capital-markets role. The only C++ work shown is a brief ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 on LabWindows/CVI, with no recent C++ experience, no Windows platform focus, no SQL Server/Oracle exposure, and no fixed-income, investment banking, or capital-markets domain background. Core stack is TypeScript/Node/DLang on web and data systems, which does not satisfy the mandatory requirements.
strengths: 10+ years of professional software engineering with senior/staff titles
Some past C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on a developer-tooling product
Strong fundamentals in testing, TDD, CI/CD, and low-latency systems (via DLang at Dunnhumby)
concerns: No substantive recent C++ experience (only ~6 months in 2014–2015) and no Windows platform focus
No fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital-markets domain experience
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; SQL listed only generically

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++/Windows experience vs. 5–10 yr requirement
No fixed income or capital-markets domain background
